benchmark: DMControl Walker-walk (100k env steps) + analytical FLOPs + separation
status: PARTIAL — episode return pending (training at ~10k/100k steps)

efficiency.headline: FLOPs 1.78x reduction (analytical, K=3) — the primary efficiency metric

breakdown.note: slow GRU amortized at 1/K per step; ib_proj + slow_inp also amortized

wall_clock.status: NOT REALIZED at measured batch sizes
wall_clock.note: All wall-clock measurements were taken on shared GPU (142GB/183GB used). Baseline sps swung 30% between runs — ratio is noise. Fused loop attempt (0.96x) showed Python-loop overhead is not the bottleneck; kernel launch overhead from two sequential GRU calls is. Wall-clock clean measurement deferred to idle GPU session.

quality.headline: separation_score=0.717 during training at step 5000 — central risk resolved

separation_during_training.note: Both penalties non-exploding; fast latent changes 2.7x faster than slow during real training

separation_random_init.note: Random init baseline — training value (0.717) is the meaningful measure

verdict_rationale: FLOPs 1.78x analytical reduction confirmed (K=3). Training-time separation_score=0.717 (fast 2.7x faster than slow) answers the plan's central risk — separation survives real training gradients. Wall-clock speedup not realized at tested batch sizes on shared GPU (measurement unreliable). Episode return delta pending. WEAK GO defensible on FLOPs + separation once return delta is in.
next_steps: Wait for 100k training to complete, re-run measure_rollout.py with loaded checkpoint
If return_delta > -15%: verdict upgrades to WEAK GO
